fault_hypothesis: Line 68 applies `int()` (truncation) to each raw share, which loses fractional parts and causes the sum to be less than `total` when shares are non-integer. The rounding/remainder adjustment should happen inside `_compute_raw_shares` or before truncation, but the plain `int()` cast discards remainders.

spec.expr: sum(int(x) for x in shares__1) == total

constraints.intent: After truncating shares to int, their sum must equal total; fails when fractional parts are discarded.
Raw shares should sum very close to total before integer conversion.

spec.expr: sum(int(x) for x in shares__1) == total or any((x - int(x)) > 0 for x in shares__1)

constraints.intent: If any share has a fractional part, plain int() will lose it and the sum will not equal total.
The returned list of integer allocations must sum exactly to total.
